texto: There's nothing more superficial to do than to paint a beautiful woman. The most beautiful portraits in art were of ugly women. If you paint Brigitte Bardot, it's a disaster. Sunsets, you have to stay away from sunsets. You paint a sunset, you are in great danger.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Fernando Botero sobre temas ligados a familia. A afirmação “There's nothing more superficial to do than to paint a beautiful woman. The most beautiful portra…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, art, beautiful, útil em momentos de beautiful e sunset.

frase_original: There's nothing more superficial to do than to paint a beautiful woman. The most beautiful portraits in art were of ugly women. If you paint Brigitte Bardot, it's a disaster. Sunsets, you have to stay away from sunsets. You paint a sunset, you are in great danger.

texto: My popularity has to do with the divorce between modern art, where everything is obscure, and the viewer who often feels he needs a professor to tell them whether it's good or not. I believe a painting has to talk directly to the viewer, with composition, color and design, without a professor to explain it.
explicacao: Citação de Fernando Botero sobre temas ligados a familia. “My popularity has to do with the divorce between modern art, where everything is obscure, and the…” articula familia, art, good em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como good e believe.

frase_original: My popularity has to do with the divorce between modern art, where everything is obscure, and the viewer who often feels he needs a professor to tell them whether it's good or not. I believe a painting has to talk directly to the viewer, with composition, color and design, without a professor to explain it.

texto: All my life, my girlfriends are always skinny. Beauty in art has nothing to do with beauty in reality. Why do you like primitive art? Because there is beauty in the deformity. Sometimes paintings that people consider realistic are not at all. Raphael figures look realistic, but in real life, they were deformed.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Fernando Botero: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “All my life, my girlfriends are always skinny. Beauty in art has nothing to do with beauty in rea…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, vida, art pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e art.

texto: Art should be an oasis: a place or refuge from the hardness of life.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Fernando Botero sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “Art should be an oasis: a place or refuge from the hardness of life.” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a vida, place, oasis, útil em momentos de place e oasis.
